title: Guarea microsepala
taxonomy: Guarea microsepala is classified within the family Meliaceae, commonly known as the mahogany family. This family consists of approximately 350 species of trees and shrubs, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Guarea itself comprises about 50 species, most of which are native to the Americas. Guarea microsepala is a species of tree that is endemic to certain regions in Central and South America. It belongs to the order Sapindales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes other families such as Rutaceae and Anacardiaceae.

intro: Guarea microsepala, also known as the micro-flowered Guarea, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The Guarea microsepala has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Guarea microsepala include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
